nodes.label: Retry-Safe Side Effects
nodes.rationale: Irreversible API operations and webhook processing must define retry, deduplication, transaction, and conflict behavior.

nodes.label: JWT Authentication Lifecycle
nodes.rationale: JWT authentication requires explicit guard, lifetime, refresh, invalidation, authorization, error, rate-limit, and test decisions rather than only a login endpoint.

nodes.label: API Contract Documentation Synchronization
nodes.rationale: Meaningful API contract changes should update documentation and tests in the same change set.

nodes.label: Safe Collection Queries
nodes.rationale: Collection endpoints need bounded pagination, allowlisted filters and sorts, stable ordering, intentional relationship loading, and index-aware query design.

nodes.label: Laravel API Production Readiness
nodes.rationale: Production readiness requires coordinated routing, validation, responses, authorization, JWT, query safety, throttling, idempotency, errors, tests, documentation, and deployment review.

nodes.label: Risk-Based Rate Limiting
nodes.rationale: Endpoints with different abuse and cost profiles require distinct limiters, keys, quotas, and consistent 429 responses.

nodes.label: Stable Laravel Resource Contracts
nodes.rationale: Explicit JsonResource, ResourceCollection, or intentional JSON:API resources prevent model leaks, lazy-loading surprises, and accidental contract changes.

nodes.label: Authentication and Authorization Boundary
nodes.rationale: Authentication middleware, policies or gates, and server-side tenant ownership checks must jointly protect API resources.

nodes.label: Safe API Error Envelope
nodes.rationale: A stable machine-readable envelope improves client reliability while preventing stack traces, SQL errors, and infrastructure details from leaking.

nodes.label: Laravel 13 Documentation Grounding
nodes.rationale: Laravel-specific changes should follow Context7 or official Laravel 13 documentation before project conventions and existing patterns are applied.

nodes.label: Resource-Oriented Versioned Routes
nodes.rationale: Predictable versioned resources, explicit transition endpoints, shallow nesting, scoped bindings, and correct API-prefix handling make routes easier to secure and maintain.

nodes.label: Evidence-Backed Laravel Review Gate
nodes.author: albifhrzq
nodes.rationale: Completion and merge readiness must be based on traced execution paths, applicable Laravel rules, fresh test evidence, contract checks, and production-risk review without modifying code during review.
